name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: Day 77. **US 10Y 4.46% MAY 15 (-0.4% 1D, +4.2% 1M, +10.1% 3M); US 2Y 3.59% (-0.3% 1D, -0.7% 1M, -0.1% 3M).** Front-end says no near-term cut, no near-term hike either. **April CPI 3.8% YoY + April PPI +1.4% m/m closed near-term Fed easing door**; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. Real-yield channel keeps gold in regime-shift unwind (gold -2.2% 1D); DXY 99.13 (+0.3% 1D) — modest dollar bid on Trump-Xi joint statement. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-regime + paper-physical bifurcation at extreme**: VIX 17.26 cycle low + S&P/NASDAQ/Dow record closes + 10Y at 4.46% holding all reinforce paper-side complacency on a day with two new kinetic incidents and an Israeli KIA. India 10Y G-Sec 7.12% (DealPlexus / CCIL May 14 — yield easing as traders awaited Trump-Xi summit outcome; CCIL monthly avg 6.95% vs 7.04% prior). **US 10Y 4.48% MAY 14 (+0.4% 1D, +5.3% 1M, +10.5% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, -0.3% 1M, +0.2% 3M).** CPI absorption — Fed easing closed (April CPI 3.8% three-year high May 12; energy +17.9% YoY contributed >40% headline gain; core +2.8% YoY). Front-end says no near-term cut, no near-term hike either. **Real-yield channel keeps gold underperforming** ($4,701.50 +0.1% 1D — modestly underperforming bear case on Trump-Xi opening). India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (month high — investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; Moody's sovereign-cut absorbed; +bps vs recent 6.96% range). DXY 98.48 (-0.0% 1D, +0.4% 1M, +1.4% 3M) — dollar failing to bid on crisis day (G5). **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime: VIX 17.87 sub-18 + S&P 500 +0.6% + 10Y at 4.48% holding all reinforce that markets see crisis as INFLATION + FLOW event** (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT financial-system stress event. PRIOR (May 9): **US 10Y 4.36% MAY 8 CLOSE (-0.6% 1D, +1.7% 1M, +4.0% 3M); US 2Y 3.60% (-0.1% 1D, +0.2% 1M).** Modest 10Y pullback on stronger-than-expected May 8 jobs print (per Reuters/Investing 'labor market resilience') reduced immediate June-cut probability; longer-end yields easing back from FOMC-driven highs. **Real-yield channel still keeping gold underperforming:** US 10Y at 4.36% kept real yields competitive vs gold; gold $4,720.40 (+0.4% 1D) underperformed on a crisis-day with May 7 destroyer attacks + May 8 second tanker disabling + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' speech. **Cross-asset confirmation of inflation-not-systemic-stress regime:** VIX cycle-low 17.08 + S&P 500 record close 7,398.93 + 10Y not making new highs all reinforce that markets see the West Asia crisis as an INFLATION + FLOW event (negative for gold via real-yield channel) NOT a financial-system stress event (which would be positive for gold + bid for 10Y). Cheniere $3.5B Q1 GAAP loss on derivative MtM is the closest thing to a financial-stress signal but is firm-specific not systemic. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0% (+6-8 bps cycle, ~+15bps from late-Apr lows, ~+25bps from pre-crisis) — rupee weakness + oil inflation + FII channel keeping pressure. DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M) modest dollar weakness. Powell verbal 'hasn't even peaked yet' on energy inflation still operative; CME FedWatch June-cut probability now low.

current.regime: Inflation + flow event regime confirmed (NOT systemic-stress event). Real yields competitive vs gold (gold underperforming on crisis days). May 8 jobs print stronger than expected reduced June-cut probability. Cheniere derivative loss firm-specific not systemic. India 10Y +6-8 bps cycle on rupee/oil/FII pressure.
